title: ComplianceReport
description: Structured output of audit_ai_deployment(). Used both as the JSON Schema for validation and as the input_schema of the submit_compliance_report tool that Claude is forced to call.

compliance_status.description: Overall verdict. 'non_compliant' requires at least one high-severity violation with clear evidence.

risk_score.description: Rough magnitude of compliance risk. 0=clean, 4-6=Art. 50 gap, 7+=Art. 5 prohibition match.

violations.description: Triggered findings, one entry per catalog violation that fired. Empty list means no violations.

violation_id.description: Exactly one of the catalog IDs (V001-V012). Do not invent new IDs.

article.description: AI Act article reference, copied verbatim from catalog (e.g., 'Article 50(1)').

evidence.description: Verbatim quote from input text (max ~200 chars). Use '[absence of required disclosure]' if the violation is an absence rather than a present quote.

explanation.description: 1-2 sentences explaining why this is a problem in this specific input.

suggested_fix.description: Concrete text the user can paste in. For V001/V002 disclosure fixes, given in the same language as the input.

deadline.description: When the obligation entered or enters into force (ISO date). Copied from catalog's in_force_since.

general_recommendations.description: Broader suggestions not tied to a specific catalog ID. May be empty.

disclaimer.description: Always: 'This is an automated first-pass check, not legal advice. Consult qualified counsel before deploying.'

audited_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when this audit was produced.

audit_version.description: Version of the audit ruleset that produced this report.
